cam_inputs.blocker: Downstream traceability work on SE-05 (RTM) is constrained by open interface requirements in SE-03 sections 4–6, which are blocked pending RF specifications from Hardware.
The remaining 15% of the SDD is intentionally held open pending resolution of ICD sections 4–6, which depend on RF subsystem specs that Hardware has not yet delivered.
cam_inputs.risk_description:
Two things, and they're both on the Hardware side.

First, I need a firm delivery date for the RF specs — not a "we're working on it," but a committed date with a named owner. Right now I'm operating on "end of next week" as an expectation, not a commitment. There's a difference, and that distinction matters when I'm making decisions about whether to hold SE-03 open or start making conservative assumptions and risk rework.

Second, if Hardware can't commit to end of next week, I need to know that now — not on Friday. If the date slips, I'd rather have two weeks of warning than two days. That lead time lets me assess whether there are any ICD sections I can close independently using preliminary data, with a formal update cycle once finals arrive. It's not ideal from a configuration control standpoint, but it may be the right trade if it keeps PDR holdable.

What I'm not able to do is manufacture progress on SE-03 sections 4 through 6, the SDD final 15%, or SE-08 without that input. Those aren't pace constraints — they're hard dependencies.

So the ask for PM action is straightforward: get Hardware in a room, get a committed date, and if that date is past end of next week, escalate it now so we can make a deliberate decision about PDR rather than having the schedule decision made for us by a late data delivery.

Has there been any direct conversation with the Hardware lead about what's actually holding up those specs? Because I don't have visibility into what's causing the delay on their end, and that matters for assessing whether this is a one-week slip risk or something larger.

cam_inputs.blocker: Sections 4-6 of the ICD cannot be completed until Hardware delivers the RF subsystem specifications, which are not yet available.
RTM progress will plateau at 60-65% until ICD sections 4-6 are closed, because RF subsystem interface definitions (SE-03) must be stable before Level 3 and below requirements can be traced.

The PDR package cannot advance materially because its primary upstream inputs (SE-03, SE-05, SE-07) are incomplete, with SE-07 barely started at 5% and the RF spec delivery from Hardware unresolved, blocking ICD closure and RTM progress.
System Safety Analysis cannot progress on RF-related hazards — the majority of the work — until ICD sections 4-6 are closed, which is blocked on Hardware RF spec delivery.
Reliability Analysis (SE-08) cannot begin until SE-03 (ICD approval) is complete, which is blocked on ICD sections 4–6, which are blocked on the undelivered Hardware RF specs.
SE-10 is blocked by incomplete upstream inputs — the RTM (SE-05) is only at 40% and the ICD (SE-03) is incomplete, both of which trace back to the undelivered Hardware RF spec.
Tool license contention on the lab cluster is preventing engineers from running required simulations, stalling progress on RF Front End Design.
cam_inputs.risk_description: Dedicated license slots on the cluster — even just two or three reserved for my team during off-peak hours would help. Or a short-term license expansion if the program can swing the cost. Either way, I need it resolved in the next week, maybe ten days max. After that, we're into slip territory on HW-05.

cam_inputs.blocker: A key engineer has been on medical leave for two weeks with approximately three more weeks expected, leaving only partial coverage from a less experienced team member.
The updated antenna aperture requirements ICD from Alice's team has not been delivered, preventing finalization of critical design sections.
HW-05 fabrication cannot start until HW-01 RF Front End Design and simulations are signed off, which is being held up by tool license contention on the lab cluster.
HW-06 fab cannot start until HW-02 design is complete, and HW-02 is delayed due to a key engineer on medical leave for approximately three weeks with only partial coverage.
HW-07 cannot begin fabrication until HW-04 design is complete, which is itself blocked on antenna aperture ICD requirements from Alice's team.

HW-08 cannot start until HW-05, HW-06, and HW-07 all complete, and those upstream tasks are each threatened by open issues: license contention on HW-01, a staffing gap on HW-02, and an unresolved external ICD dependency on HW-04.
HW-09 (ESS) is blocked upstream by HW-08 (unit testing), which is itself stalled due to three unresolved open issues: cluster license, backfill engineer, and ICD date.

Three open issues are driving risk to Hardware Acceptance Test completion: cluster license contention on HW-01, a staffing gap on HW-02, and an ICD hold on HW-04, with the ICD hold on the antenna path being the highest-risk item due to no schedule buffer.
UI Development cannot begin until SW-02 completes and SW-03 advances further, as these predecessor tasks provide the architecture and backend hooks required to stand up the UI.
SW-08 has a dependency on hardware availability for the integrated test bed, which is a coordination risk with the HW lead.
Hardware availability is a dependency risk for SQT, as it requires a stable, representative test environment shared with the HW team.
The second report is in draft and cannot be finalized until outstanding inputs are received from several CAMs.
A few CAMs have not yet closed out their actuals, which is holding up finalization of the current report.
Several subcontractors have been slow providing their schedule data, causing delays in completing the first formal performance review cycle.
